text.insert: The state-shape component owns one block type,

. A block carries a recursive field tree — name, type, optionality, meaning — an optional link to the defining source symbol, and an optional JSON example instance rendered beside the tree. Model code lives in
It is the state carrier of the corpus documentation doctrine: a state-shape block carries the shape of state and an example instance side by side, an
lists the operations that change or query it, and annotated
blocks hold the source evidence. State first, then operations; a code block is for material that is not an instance of the shape — a real source listing.
A live instance: a compact theme shape beside its linked JSON example pane. The shape documenting this block's own state sits under State Schema below.

fields.name: headerBg
fields.description: Header row background; one value or a per-mode pair.

text.insert: contract element: all state lives in typed props, defined by
. The type carries no delta text (

fields.name: name
fields.description: Bold header-line label; usually the type name.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Prose summary of the shape; not part of the markdown render.

fields.name: source
fields.description: Defining source location; renders as an em-dash suffix on the header line.

fields.name: path
fields.description: Path of the defining source file.

fields.name: symbol
fields.description: Symbol within that file; renders as a #symbol suffix.

fields.name: fields
fields.description: The recursive field tree, in document order.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Field name; unique among siblings — dot-path addressing depends on it.

fields.name: type
fields.description: Type text, rendered after a colon.

fields.name: required
fields.description: false = optional and renders a ? suffix; omitted or true reads as required.

fields.name: fields
fields.description: Child fields, rendered two spaces deeper; the node recurses.

fields.name: example
fields.description: JSON text of an example instance of this shape; renders as the linked example pane.

text.insert: is the shared recursive node in
A custom check past the schema enforces sibling-unique field names at every level — dot-path addressing depends on it — and that a present
skip malformed entries instead of throwing.
contract element, defined one file each in
. Params validate against each action's TypeBox schema before
runs; every action returns a shallow props patch —
or an omitted path names the root
inserts a field under the parent named by
defaults to the end, so document order is curated order and survives edits. Duplicate names — among the target siblings or inside the inserted subtree — are rejected.
sets or clears the example; the string must parse as JSON,

operations.name: state-shape.addField
operations.description: Insert a field ({ name, type?, required?, description?, fields? }) under the parent named by path; index defaults to the end.

params.name: path
params.description: Dot-path of the PARENT field; "" or omitted inserts into the root fields array.

params.name: index
params.description: Insert position among the parent's fields; default end.

operations.name: state-shape.updateField
operations.description: Patch the field at path (rename via patch.name; null clears type/required/description; patch.fields replaces the subtree, null removes it).

params.name: path
params.description: Dot-path of the field to patch, e.g. "operations.params".

params.name: patch
params.description: Partial field; patch.name renames, null clears.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Rename; must stay unique among siblings.

fields.name: fields
fields.description: Replaces the subtree; null removes it.

operations.name: state-shape.removeField
operations.description: Remove the field at path, together with its entire subtree.

params.name: path
params.description: Dot-path of the field to remove, e.g. "operations.params".

operations.name: state-shape.setExample
operations.description: Set the JSON example instance rendered beside the field tree (example must parse as JSON; null clears it).

params.name: example
params.description: JSON text of an example instance of this shape; null clears the example.

text.insert: , wired through the descriptor in the same folder. The render is a quiet bordered two-pane card — no title bar, never a language tag, state is always JSON: the structure tree left and, when
parses as JSON, a line-numbered example pane right.
An optional header row: bold mono shape name, muted
Top-level fields render as hairline-divided groups: bold mono name, mono type, a muted
, description as a smaller second line.
Nested fields sit behind a light left rule — one step per depth — with no dividers of their own.
The example pretty-prints through the shared
JSON token toning is deterministic — a tiny line tokenizer over the canonical print, no highlight.js.
Field rows and example lines link by field dot-path; array indices normalize away, so a field matches its path at every array position.
Hover or pin paints the field's full extent in both panes; activating an ancestor lights its whole brace-to-brace range.
Without an example the card is the single-pane tree — nothing linkable.
strictly: any malformed entry renders the invalid-block placeholder.
is read tolerantly: present-but-invalid JSON falls back to the single-pane tree; schema validation reports it at authoring time.
In the editor the block is a ProseMirror atom leaf (
output as the reader. No slash-menu entry; instances enter through agent ops or existing content.
contract element: a deterministic markdown projection in
appended when a source is present; without a name the source stands alone as
Then a bare fence, one line per field in the shared field-line grammar: two-space indent per nesting depth,
is present, a blank line and a
; the tolerant read drops a non-JSON example, so a malformed prop renders no fence rather than crashing.
Projected, the live Example above is the header line

accent: string  # Accent rule color.
headerBg?: string | { light, dark }  # Header row background; one value or a per-mode pair.
slider?: object  # Numeric control metadata.
  kind: "color" | "length" | "number"
  max?: number
). Every value is one string for both modes or a
. Ten tokens — nine colors and one length:

rows: Shape name in the header row
Muted detail text — optionality markers and the source ref
Hairlines: header underline, top-level row dividers, pane split
Description text — header and field rows
Top-level row vertical padding; length slider, 4–16 px, default 9 px

The returned props patch executes through the existing

Structural edits ride the generic ops —
